what_it_does: A posted noticeboard note by Tim that replies to another item, discussing mobile UI ergonomics, a proposed bottom-right control cluster, reusable component architecture, and expectations that channel notes are persisted and typed.
observations: Tim agrees with a prior critique that a top-left hamburger is a poor one-handed thumb target.
He states a principle: frequent actions belong at the bottom/thumb zone, rare/deliberate actions may live at the top.
He rejects both a top-left hamburger and a conventional bottom bar.
He proposes an unconventional bottom-right control area shaped around a 'V' with up to four additional same-component slots, possibly forming an L shape.
He wants the slots to be a reusable component/template so their contents can be swapped easily.
He mentions 'modes' should be a registry and that two modes have been identified for this app.
He references a 'composition stream' and its Supabase backend.
He expects these comments are typed to him and persisted/filterable in the future.
standouts: The proposed bottom-right V + interchangeable slot cluster as a reusable component.
The insistence that channel notes must be persisted and typed to the author.
The explicit rejection of both top-left hamburger and standard bottom bar.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history.
Links to a prior item via commented_on relationship.
concerns: The note mixes UI design, component architecture, backend references, and persistence policy in one unstructured message.
It contains typos and grammatical noise that may obscure intent.
It assumes persistence/typing behavior without confirming it is implemented.
No explicit action items or acceptance criteria are listed.
notes: Tim's design note arguing for thumb-zone controls, a reusable bottom-right V-slot component, and raising expectations about persistence of channel memory.
questions: What exactly is the 'V' component and its intended iconography?
What are the two identified modes in the registry?
What is the 'composition stream' architecture and how does it relate to this UI?
Are comments actually persisted and typed to the author_session as assumed?
What specific actions or menus are meant to occupy the four slots around the V?
What are 'blocks', 'gruff view', and 'grass builder' in this context?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a point/comment on a prior note; it does that, but also expands into component architecture, mode registry, and persistence policy, making it broader than a focused design point.
apparent_themes: Persistence and auditability of channel memory
intention_signals: To refine the application's chrome so it is ergonomic for one-handed use, define a reusable bottom-right control cluster, and ensure the design rationale is captured and retrievable in the channel memory.
novelty: Proposes an unconventional L-shaped bottom-right control cluster centered on a 'V' with up to four interchangeable same-component slots, explicitly rejecting both the top-left hamburger and the standard bottom bar.
contribution: Contributes a concrete ergonomic principle (frequency determines altitude), a reusable slot-based UI component concept, and a persistence requirement for channel notes.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim critiquing top-left hamburger placement, proposing a bottom-right V-shaped reusable control cluster, and insisting that channel comments be persisted and typed.
intention_for_embedding: To record Tim's UI/UX rationale and component architecture ideas for the dragnet-development channel.
